ALL SPECIALTIES / PHARMACY TECHNICIAN / WASHINGTON

Photo: Unsplash / Unsplash

Washington Pharmacy Technician Registration

Washington Pharmacy Technician license requirements, fees, and steps.

Last updated 2026-03-18 · Sources verified

  • New Applicant
  • Reciprocity
  • Renewal

New Applicant

Pharmacy Technician applications in Washington run through Washington Pharmacy Quality Assurance Commission. Use the checklist below before starting your application.

Required documents

  • Official Transcripts - Sent directly from your educational institution.
  • Exam Score Report - Official verification of passing the PTCB (Pharmacy Technician Certification Board) or ExCPT exam.

Key timeline factors

  • Board processing 4 to 6 Weeks
  • Fingerprinting Required - complete this early to avoid delays.
  • Application completeness Missing or mismatched documents are the most common reason timelines extend.

Reciprocity

Pharmacy technicians registered in another state may apply for registration in Washington by submitting a new application to the Washington Pharmacy Quality Assurance Commission. There is no interstate compact for pharmacy technicians. Applicants must provide proof of current national certification (PTCB or ExCPT), complete any Washington-specific requirements, and pass a criminal background check. Processing typically takes 4 to 6 Weeks.

  1. STEP 1

    Meet the Eligibility Requirements

    You must be 18 years of age, have a high school diploma or equivalent, and have good moral character.

    Fee: $140 · Timeline: 4 to 6 Weeks

  2. STEP 2

    Complete a Training Program

    Complete a Board-approved pharmacy technician training program or employer-based training program.

    Fee: $140 · Timeline: 4 to 6 Weeks

  3. STEP 3

    Register for a SecureAccess Washington (SAW) Account

    Go to secureaccess.wa.gov and create your SAW account. You'll set your own user ID and password. Check your email and click the activation link to complete registration. If you have issues, contact WATech at 855-928-3241.

    Fee: $140 · Timeline: 4 to 6 Weeks

  4. STEP 4

    Link to HELMS Through SAW

    Log into SAW, select 'Add a New Service,' choose 'Department of Health,' then scroll to 'Health Professional and Facility Licensing (HELMS)' and select 'Apply.' Complete the identity verification questions using your full legal name.

    Fee: $140 · Timeline: 4 to 6 Weeks

  5. STEP 5

    Submit Your Application in HELMS

    From MyServices, open HELMS, search for Pharmacy Technician, and click 'Start a New Application.' Complete all required fields. Note: after 14 days of inactivity your application is deleted — log back in to keep it active.

    Fee: $140 · Timeline: 4 to 6 Weeks

  6. STEP 6

    Pay the Application Fee

    Pay the $140 credential fee plus a 2.5% convenience fee online. Payment must be in U.S. funds from a U.S. financial institution. Ensure your billing address matches what your bank has on file.

    Fee: $140 · Timeline: 4 to 6 Weeks

Fees and Costs

Application fee
$140
Background check
$35-$50 (IdentoGO fingerprint fee, paid to vendor)
Jurisprudence exam
$0
Total estimated cost
$175-$190 (application + fingerprinting)

Always confirm current fees on the board site before submitting.

Board Contact

Board
Washington Pharmacy Quality Assurance Commission
Phone
360-236-4700
Website
doh.wa.gov/licenses-permits-and-certificates/professions-new-renew-or-update/pharmacy-professions/licensing-information
Application page
Open application

Renewal

Pharmacy Technician renewal in Washington follows the board cycle below.

Renewal cycle
Every 2 years, on or before your birthday
Renewal fee
$140
Continuing education
20 hours per renewal cycle

Frequently Asked Questions

How much does a Washington pharmacy technician license cost?

The Washington Department of Health lists a $140 original pharmacy technician credential fee and a $140 renewal fee.

Is national certification required?

Yes, passing the PTCB (Pharmacy Technician Certification Board) or ExCPT exam is a standard requirement for initial licensure across almost all states.

Does Washington have reciprocity for pharmacy technicians?

No interstate compact currently covers pharmacy technician practice in Washington. To work in Washington, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

How long does it take to transfer my pharmacy technician license to Washington?

Washington does not publish a public pharmacy technician processing timeline on its licensing information page. Use the board application tools and provider search to check status.

Can I start working in Washington while my endorsement application is processing?

Contact the Washington Pharmacy Quality Assurance Commission to determine whether a temporary or trainee permit is available while your endorsement application is being processed. Requirements vary, and some applicants may need to wait for full approval before beginning work in Washington.

At a glance

Endorsement fee

$140

Processing time

4 to 6 Weeks

Fingerprinting

Required

Jurisprudence exam

Required

Temporary license

Available · See board

National exam

See board

Compact

No · plan on endorsement

Renewal

Every 2 years, on or before your birthday · $140

Licensing board

Washington Pharmacy Quality Assurance Commission
